issues.title: Integer overflow in DecryptContentCore allocation
issues.problem: When decrypting CMS EnvelopedData, the code adds blockSize to encryptedContent.dataLen without checking for overflow. An attacker could craft a CMS message with encryptedContent.dataLen close to UINT32_MAX, causing integer overflow and allocating a buffer that is too small, leading to heap buffer overflow during decryption.

issues.title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_DecryptUpdate buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds blockSize to input->dataLen and then adds curOutLen without checking for overflow. An attacker could provide large input data sizes to cause integer overflow, leading to undersized buffer allocation and heap overflow.

issues.title: Integer overflow in TransferAccumulatedData buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds curOutLen and accDataLen without checking for overflow, which could lead to undersized buffer allocation during data transfer in the streaming decryption path.

issues.title: DecryptInit reports success after decryption context setup fails
issues.problem: EnvelopedData_DecryptInit() clears the CEK when SetDecryCtx() fails, but then still marks the object as initialized and returns success. Callers will continue with a half-initialized decrypt state and only fail later in Update/Final.

issues.title: Generator emits EnvelopedData with an empty RecipientInfos set
issues.problem: RFC 5652 section 6.1 requires RecipientInfos ::= SET SIZE (1..MAX) OF RecipientInfo. This code returns success for count == 0, so encryption can generate an invalid CMS object that cannot be decrypted and that ParseRecipientList() would reject.

issues.title: KEM KEK derivation ignores the CMS provider context
issues.problem: EncryptCekForKemri() derives the KEK from kemri->libCtx and kemri->attrName, but those runtime fields are never populated when KEM recipients are created (CMS_KemriNew) or parsed (ParseKemri). ML-KEM EnvelopedData therefore falls back to a NULL/default provider context and breaks non-default-provider flows.

issues.title: Parser accepts IV-based content-encryption algorithms without parameters
issues.problem: For algorithms such as AES-CBC, AlgorithmIdentifier.parameters carries the IV and is required. This branch silently accepts a missing parameter block and leaves item->algParams = NULL, so malformed input is only rejected later, or worse, is decrypted with a NULL/zero-length IV.

issues.title: Decrypt final trusts CRYPT_PARAM_DECODE_BUFFER_DATA without type checking
issues.problem: The decrypt-final path only checks that p->value is non-NULL and then casts it to BSL_Buffer *. A caller can pass the right key with the wrong valueType or valueLen and trigger an invalid cast and write into unrelated memory.

issues.title: EnvelopedData feature omits required crypto and X509 dependencies
issues.problem: hitls_cms_envelopeddata.c uses X509 cert/CRL parse/generate helpers (HITLS_X509_CertCtrl, HITLS_X509_SetNameList, HITLS_X509_ParseAttrList), cipher code (CRYPT_EAL_Cipher*), HKDF (CRYPT_EAL_KdfDerive), RSA encryption/decryption (CRYPT_EAL_PkeyEncrypt/Decrypt), and ML-KEM, but the feature dependency only enables HITLS_PKI_CMS and HITLS_BSL_ASN1. Feature combinations that turn on HITLS_PKI_CMS_ENVELOPEDDATA without those subfeatures can build into missing symbols or a nonfunctional binary.
issues.fix:
hitls_define_dependency(HITLS_PKI_CMS_ENVELOPEDDATA     DEPS HITLS_PKI_CMS HITLS_BSL_ASN1
    HITLS_PKI_X509_CRT_PARSE HITLS_CRYPTO_CIPHER HITLS_CRYPTO_HKDF
    HITLS_CRYPTO_RSA_ENCRYPT HITLS_CRYPTO_RSA_DECRYPT HITLS_CRYPTO_RSAES_PKCSV15
    HITLS_CRYPTO_PKEY_KEM
)

issues.title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_EncryptUpdate buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds blockSize to input->dataLen and then adds currentLen without checking for overflow. While this is in the encryption path (not parsing), it could still cause issues with very large inputs.

issues.title: Integer overflow in EncryptContentCore buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds blockSize to plaintext->dataLen without checking for overflow. While this is in the encryption path, it could cause issues with very large plaintext inputs.

issues.title: Potential buffer underflow in ParseRecipientList
issues.problem: The code subtracts valueLen from buffLen without verifying that valueLen <= buffLen. If BSL_ASN1_DecodeLen returns a valid valueLen but valueLen > buffLen due to malformed ASN.1 data, this would cause unsigned integer underflow. The BSL_ASN1_DecodeLen function does not guarantee that the decoded length fits in the remaining buffer.

issues.title: Integer overflow in FinalizeEncry buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds blockSize to currentLen without checking for overflow during encryption finalization.

issues.title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_DecryptFinal buffer allocation
issues.problem: The code adds blockSize to currentLen without checking for overflow during decryption finalization.
